behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a module-handoff/wiring check: request builder -> API client payload. A unit test of a helper can miss dropped/renamed fields at integration boundaries. System was considered but rejected because the behavior text only requires that outbound construction includes labels, not verification of a real provider response contract or external round-trip side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Run the classifier with an in-process fake API client and assert the captured outbound request payload contains the full configured taxonomy list on every call path.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure can occur at response parsing + normalization handoff across modules, not just in a single pure function. Real infra is not required, and correctness does not depend on model quality; a controlled fake response is sufficient. Agentic/System were rejected because behavior text is about deterministic guardrail mapping of an out-of-taxonomy value, not real-model judgment or real-network contract validation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier + response parser/normalizer with a fake provider response containing an invalid label and assert caller receives exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is a determinism property of normalization logic for identical input; no external boundary, OS/network, or model non-determinism is needed. Higher levels add little new reality signal for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization repeatedly with identical raw response fixtures and assert identical final labels each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI outputs and potential model/provider variability. Stubs cannot reveal real non-determinism, so unit/integration would give false confidence. Workflow was considered but rejected because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute repeated real provider calls with the same input under controlled settings; record provider payload/label each run and assert label stability except when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration between token counting/policy and dispatch gating; unit tests of each piece can miss handoff bugs (e.g., request still sent after reject decision). System was considered but rejected because behavior text says enforcement happens before dispatch, so no real external round-trip is required. Agentic rejected because correctness is policy enforcement, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Test end-to-end inside process: feed oversized inputs, verify truncate/reject policy outcome, and verify dispatcher was called or blocked accordingly.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real external call and round-trip observation of provider-reported usage fields; mocked usage can hide contract/tokenization mismatches. It triggers System TRIAD (real external happy-path contract + externally visible readback). Agentic was considered but rejected because assertion targets usage accounting/contract compliance, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Make real API calls with representative documents and assert usage reported by the provider response stays within configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is retry/backoff orchestration across modules (error parser, scheduler/backoff, retry loop). This can be validated with deterministic simulated 429/over-limit signals in-process. System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not explicitly require proving the real provider’s live error contract; it specifies behavior upon a rate-limit signal. Agentic rejected because no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a controllable fake provider that returns rate-limit signals then success; assert module waits per policy, retries, and ultimately returns success instead of surfacing immediate error.
